item.title: [7] Topological constraints on the electronic band structure of a hexagonal lattice in a magnetic field
item.description:
Last authors: Qi Gao, Wei Chen (陈薇)

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on topological electronic band structure in hexagonal materials under magnetic field; relevant.

Tags: #topological #bandstructure #graphene

Abstract: Author(s): Qi Gao and Wei Chen (陈薇) The impact of projective lattice symmetry on electronic band structures has attracted significant attention in recent years, particularly in light of growing experimental studies of two-dimensional hexagonal materials in magnetic fields. Yet, most theoretical work to date has focused on the square l… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205118] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qi Gao and Wei Chen (陈薇)

The impact of projective lattice symmetry on electronic band structures has attracted significant attention in recent years, particularly in light of growing experimental studies of two-dimensional hexagonal materials in magnetic fields. Yet, most theoretical work to date has focused on the square l…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205118] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Topological constraints on the electronic band structure of a hexagonal lattice in a magnetic field
item.dc:creator: Qi Gao, Wei Chen (陈薇)

item.title: [8] Orbital mixing and strong Hund's coupling stabilize spin order in the van der Waals ferromagnet CrI3
item.description:
Last authors: R. Ernstorfer, T. Pincelli

Authors: A. De Vita, S. Stavrić, R. Sant, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of a real van der Waals ferromagnet with implications for electronic and spin order.

Tags: #vanderWaals #ferromagnet #2Dmaterials #magnetism #stronglycorrelatedelectronsystems

Abstract: Author(s): A. De Vita, S. Stavrić, R. Sant, N. B. Brookes, I. Vobornik, G. Panaccione, S. Picozzi, M. Wolf, L. Rettig, R. Ernstorfer, and T. Pincelli Recent years have seen a vast increase in research into van der Waals magnetic materials. In many of these systems, magnetism is introduced via light $3d$ transition metal elements, combined with chalcogenides or halogens. Despite the great technological promise in the field of spintronics, the conn… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205120] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. De Vita, S. Stavrić, R. Sant, N. B. Brookes, I. Vobornik, G. Panaccione, S. Picozzi, M. Wolf, L. Rettig, R. Ernstorfer, and T. Pincelli

Recent years have seen a vast increase in research into van der Waals magnetic materials. In many of these systems, magnetism is introduced via light 3d transition metal elements, combined with chalcogenides or halogens. Despite the great technological promise in the field of spintronics, the connec…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205120] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Orbital mixing and strong Hund's coupling stabilize spin order in the van der Waals ferromagnet CrI3
item.dc:creator: A. De Vita, S. Stavrić, R. Sant, et al.

item.title: [9] Room-temperature intrinsic anomalous Hall effect and robust half-metallicity in the quaternary Heusler alloy CoRuFeSi
item.description:
Last authors: V. Kanchana, Tapas Paramanik

Authors: Manikantha Panda, Sonali S Pradhan, Prabuddha Kant Mishra, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental anomalous Hall effect in a Heusler alloy; very direct interest.

Tags: #anomalousHall #magnetictopologicalmaterials #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Manikantha Panda, Sonali S Pradhan, Prabuddha Kant Mishra, Alapan Bera, Rosni Roy, Soumik Mukhopadhyay, Rajib Mondal, V. Kanchana, and Tapas Paramanik We report a comprehensive study of the magnetic, magnetotransport, and Hall transport properties of the quaternary Heusler alloy CoRuFeSi. The compound has a saturation magnetization of 4.21 ${µ}_{B}$/f.u. and is classified as a soft ferromagnet at room temperature. The longitudinal resistivity exhi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205122] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Manikantha Panda, Sonali S Pradhan, Prabuddha Kant Mishra, Alapan Bera, Rosni Roy, Soumik Mukhopadhyay, Rajib Mondal, V. Kanchana, and Tapas Paramanik

We report a comprehensive study of the magnetic, magnetotransport, and Hall transport properties of the quaternary Heusler alloy CoRuFeSi. The compound has a saturation magnetization of 4.21 µB/f.u. and is classified as a soft ferromagnet at room temperature. The longitudinal resistivity exhibits an…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205122] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Room-temperature intrinsic anomalous Hall effect and robust half-metallicity in the quaternary Heusler alloy CoRuFeSi

item.title: [9] Physics of the spin-orbit coupling at the surface of the model topological insulators Bi2Se3 and Bi2Te3: Theory and experiments
item.description:
Last authors: F. Cilento, H. Ebert, J. Braun, and F. Parmigiani

Authors: D. Puntel, F. Sammartino, W. Bronsch, S. Peli, F. Cilento, H. Ebert, J. Braun, and F. Parmigiani

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental and theoretical study of surface states in model topological insulators; highly relevant.

Tags: #topologicalinsulator #spin-orbitcoupling #surfacestates #experiments

Abstract: Author(s): D. Puntel, F. Sammartino, W. Bronsch, S. Peli, F. Cilento, H. Ebert, J. Braun, and F. Parmigiani The dispersion of topological surface states in three-dimensional topological insulators (TIs) is commonly attributed to the atomic spin-orbit coupling (SOC) of the constituent elements. Whether the observable surface-state dispersion reflects this bare atomic value, or instead an effective SOC reno… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205308] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): D. Puntel, F. Sammartino, W. Bronsch, S. Peli, F. Cilento, H. Ebert, J. Braun, and F. Parmigiani

The dispersion of topological surface states in three-dimensional topological insulators (TIs) is commonly attributed to the atomic spin-orbit coupling (SOC) of the constituent elements. Whether the observable surface-state dispersion reflects this bare atomic value, or instead an effective SOC reno…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205308] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Physics of the spin-orbit coupling at the surface of the model topological insulators Bi2Se3 and Bi2Te3: Theory and experiments

item.title: [7] Color degeneracy of competing orders near topological defect cores in planar quadratic band touching systems
item.description:
Last authors: Bitan Roy

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of topological phenomena in Bernal bilayer graphene; relevant for broader interests.

Tags: #graphene #topological #superconductivity #theory

Abstract: Author(s): Bitan Roy We study two-dimensional fermionic systems, displaying quadratic band touching in the normal state, in the presence of vortices and skyrmions of insulating and superconducting masses in the ordered phase. A prototypical example of such systems is the Bernal bilayer graphene that supports eight zero-… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205410] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bitan Roy

We study two-dimensional fermionic systems, displaying quadratic band touching in the normal state, in the presence of vortices and skyrmions of insulating and superconducting masses in the ordered phase. A prototypical example of such systems is the Bernal bilayer graphene that supports eight zero-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205410] Published Fri May 08, 2026

item.title: [7] Spectroscopic, electronic, and magnetic signatures of cation disorder in Ca3R3Ta2Ti7O26.5 (R=Dy3+, Nd3+) pyrochlores elucidated using ab initio computations and crystal-field calculations
item.description:
Last authors: Yau Yuen Yeung, Czesław Rudowicz

Authors: Dinabandhu Halder, Yatramohan Jana, Riti Ghosh, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of electronic and magnetic properties in pyrochlores using ab initio calculations.

Tags: #electronicstructure #magnetism #DFT #stronglycorrelatedelectronsystems

Abstract: Author(s): Dinabandhu Halder, Yatramohan Jana, Riti Ghosh, Shankhanil Sarkar, Suvashree Mukherjee, S. Masilla Moses Kennedy, V. Rathina Mala, Vaibhav Chauhan, Rajnikant Upadhyay, Chandan Upadhyay, Nandkishor Pal, Yau Yuen Yeung, and Czesław Rudowicz Herein, we report spectroscopic, electronic, and magnetic investigation of the mixed-cation pyrochlores $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{a}}_{3}{R}_{3}\mathrm{T}{\mathrm{a}}_{2}\mathrm{T}{\mathrm{i}}_{7}{\mathrm{O}}_{26.5}$ ($R={\mathrm{Dy}}^{3+}, {\mathrm{Nd}}^{3+}$), elucidating key signatures of A- and B-site… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184205] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dinabandhu Halder, Yatramohan Jana, Riti Ghosh, Shankhanil Sarkar, Suvashree Mukherjee, S. Masilla Moses Kennedy, V. Rathina Mala, Vaibhav Chauhan, Rajnikant Upadhyay, Chandan Upadhyay, Nandkishor Pal, Yau Yuen Yeung, and Czesław Rudowicz

Herein, we report spectroscopic, electronic, and magnetic investigation of the mixed-cation pyrochlores Ca3R3Ta2Ti7O26.5 (R=Dy3+, Nd3+), elucidating key signatures of A- and B-site cation disorder and oxygen nonstoichiometry. Structural characterization, Raman and infrared spectroscopy, complemented…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184205] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Spectroscopic, electronic, and magnetic signatures of cation disorder in Ca3R3Ta2Ti7O26.5 (R=Dy3+, Nd3+) pyrochlores elucidated using ab initio computations and crystal-field calculations
item.dc:creator: Dinabandhu Halder, Yatramohan Jana, Riti Ghosh, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Inhomogeneous, disordered, and partially ordered systems

item.title: [7] Theory of nonlinear spectroscopy of quantum magnets
item.description:
Last authors: Michael Knap, Johannes Knolle

Authors: Anubhav Srivastava, Stefan Birnkammer, GiBaik Sim, Michael Knap, Johannes Knolle

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory with experimental implications for spectroscopy of quantum magnets in a specific material.

Tags: #quantummagnets #spectroscopy #theory

Abstract: Author(s): Anubhav Srivastava, Stefan Birnkammer, GiBaik Sim, Michael Knap, and Johannes Knolle Here, the authors demonstrate that the two-dimensional coherent spectroscopic (2DCS) response of quantum magnets is influenced not only by magnetization couplings but also by magnetoelectric and polarization couplings. The authors incorporate these couplings into a minimal model for CoNb 2 O 6 and show that new response features provide information about exotic excitations. An experimental protocol is proposed to detect the coupling between electric field and spin degrees of freedom, and the role of symmetries for the nonlinear response is investigated. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184421] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Anubhav Srivastava, Stefan Birnkammer, GiBaik Sim, Michael Knap, and Johannes Knolle

Here, the authors demonstrate that the two-dimensional coherent spectroscopic (2DCS) response of quantum magnets is influenced not only by magnetization couplings but also by magnetoelectric and polarization couplings. The authors incorporate these couplings into a minimal model for CoNb2O6 and show that new response features provide information about exotic excitations. An experimental protocol is proposed to detect the coupling between electric field and spin degrees of freedom, and the role of symmetries for the nonlinear response is investigated.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184421] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Theory of nonlinear spectroscopy of quantum magnets
item.dc:creator: Anubhav Srivastava, Stefan Birnkammer, GiBaik Sim, Michael Knap, Johannes Knolle
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Light-induced magnetization dynamics in the chiral magnet MnSi
item.description:
Last authors: David Mandrus, Denis Karaiskaj

Authors: Arup Barua, Sean Knapp, Jacob Gayles, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of magnetization dynamics in a chiral quantum magnet (MnSi).

Tags: #magnetism #chiralmagnet #experiments #skyrmions

Abstract: Author(s): Arup Barua, Sean Knapp, Jacob Gayles, Tobias Weber, Theodoros Adamantopoulos, Yuriy Mokrousov, Stephen A. McGill, Shirin Mozaffari, Matthew Cothrine, David Mandrus, and Denis Karaiskaj We perform time-resolved magneto-optical Kerr effect measurements and record the magnetization dynamics for external magnetic fields along various symmetry axes of the chiral magnet MnSi. The different orientations of the magnetic field result in very different magnetization dynamics. For magnetic f… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184424] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Arup Barua, Sean Knapp, Jacob Gayles, Tobias Weber, Theodoros Adamantopoulos, Yuriy Mokrousov, Stephen A. McGill, Shirin Mozaffari, Matthew Cothrine, David Mandrus, and Denis Karaiskaj

We perform time-resolved magneto-optical Kerr effect measurements and record the magnetization dynamics for external magnetic fields along various symmetry axes of the chiral magnet MnSi. The different orientations of the magnetic field result in very different magnetization dynamics. For magnetic f…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184424] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Light-induced magnetization dynamics in the chiral magnet MnSi
item.dc:creator: Arup Barua, Sean Knapp, Jacob Gayles, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: [7] Chromium chalcohalide Janus monolayer ferromagnets with perpendicular magnetic anisotropy and high Curie temperature
item.description:
Last authors: A. Arnau, M. M. Otrokov

Authors: M. Bosnar, J. M. Lendinez, A. Yu. Vyazovskaya, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Computational prediction of 2D magnetic materials with relevant properties for user.

Tags: #monolayer #ferromagnet #2Dmaterials #DFT #materialsdiscovery

Abstract: Author(s): M. Bosnar, J. M. Lendinez, A. Yu. Vyazovskaya, I. Yu. Sklyadneva, R. Heid, S. V. Eremeev, U. Atxitia, S. Gallego, E. V. Chulkov, A. Arnau, and M. M. Otrokov Using density functional theory, we revisit the magnetic properties of a recently proposed family of noncentrosymmetric two-dimensional magnetic materials, chromium chalcohalide monolayers, $\mathrm{Cr}XY$ ($X=\mathrm{S},\mathrm{Se},\mathrm{Te}$; $Y=\mathrm{Cl},\mathrm{Br},\mathrm{I}$). These system… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184425] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. Bosnar, J. M. Lendinez, A. Yu. Vyazovskaya, I. Yu. Sklyadneva, R. Heid, S. V. Eremeev, U. Atxitia, S. Gallego, E. V. Chulkov, A. Arnau, and M. M. Otrokov

Using density functional theory, we revisit the magnetic properties of a recently proposed family of noncentrosymmetric two-dimensional magnetic materials, chromium chalcohalide monolayers, CrXY (X=S,Se,Te; Y=Cl,Br,I). These systems consist of three atomic planes stacked in the X–Cr–Y sequence, whic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184425] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Chromium chalcohalide Janus monolayer ferromagnets with perpendicular magnetic anisotropy and high Curie temperature
item.dc:creator: M. Bosnar, J. M. Lendinez, A. Yu. Vyazovskaya, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Statistical characterization of the spin Hall magnetoresistance in YIG/Pt heterostructures
item.description:
Last authors: Michaela Lammel, Sebastian T. B. Goennenwein

Authors: Denise Reustlen, Sebastian Sailler, Davina U. Schmidt, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of spin transport in a magnetic heterostructure; relevant to spintronics.

Tags: #heterostructure #ferromagnet #spintransport

Abstract: Author(s): Denise Reustlen, Sebastian Sailler, Davina U. Schmidt, Rony Werner, Richard Schlitz, Michaela Lammel, and Sebastian T. B. Goennenwein The spin Hall magnetoresistance (SMR) is a sensitive probe for interfacial spin currents in heavy metal/ferromagnet bilayers. In a statistical experimental approach, the authors compare here the SMR of hundreds of Hall bars on a single sample, observing SMR amplitude variations by up to 10%. Moreover, the average SMR on several nominally identical samples differs by up to 35%. Spatial variations of the spin mixing conductance should thus be carefully considered when analysing variations of the SMR amplitude. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184427] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Denise Reustlen, Sebastian Sailler, Davina U. Schmidt, Rony Werner, Richard Schlitz, Michaela Lammel, and Sebastian T. B. Goennenwein

The spin Hall magnetoresistance (SMR) is a sensitive probe for interfacial spin currents in heavy metal/ferromagnet bilayers. In a statistical experimental approach, the authors compare here the SMR of hundreds of Hall bars on a single sample, observing SMR amplitude variations by up to 10%. Moreover, the average SMR on several nominally identical samples differs by up to 35%. Spatial variations of the spin mixing conductance should thus be carefully considered when analysing variations of the SMR amplitude.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184427] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Statistical characterization of the spin Hall magnetoresistance in YIG/Pt heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Denise Reustlen, Sebastian Sailler, Davina U. Schmidt, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Microscopic magnetism of A(TiO)Cu4(PO4)4 (A = Ba, Pb, Sr): 31P and 63,65Cu NMR study
item.description:
Last authors: Kenta Kimura, Raivo Stern

Authors: Riho Rästa, Ivo Heinmaa, Joosep Link, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental NMR study of microscopic magnetism in a specific quantum material.

Tags: #magnetism #antiferromagnet #spectroscopy #NMR

Abstract: Author(s): Riho Rästa, Ivo Heinmaa, Joosep Link, Yusuke Kousaka, Tsuyoshi Kimura, Yoshihiko Ihara, Kenta Kimura, and Raivo Stern We report a comprehensive NMR study of the chiral square-cupola antiferromagnet $\mathrm{Pb}(\mathrm{TiO}){\mathrm{Cu}}_{4}{({\mathrm{PO}}_{4})}_{4}$ and compare its microscopic hyperfine and local-field parameters with the Ba/Sr analogs in the $A(\mathrm{TiO}){\mathrm{Cu}}_{4}{({\mathrm{PO}}_{4})}_… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184428] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Riho Rästa, Ivo Heinmaa, Joosep Link, Yusuke Kousaka, Tsuyoshi Kimura, Yoshihiko Ihara, Kenta Kimura, and Raivo Stern

We report a comprehensive NMR study of the chiral square-cupola antiferromagnet Pb(TiO)Cu4(PO4)4 and compare its microscopic hyperfine and local-field parameters with the Ba/Sr analogs in the A(TiO)Cu4(PO4)4 family. Above TN≃6.7K, the P31 Knight shift tracks the bulk susceptibility and yields nearly…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184428] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Microscopic magnetism of A(TiO)Cu4(PO4)4 (A = Ba, Pb, Sr): 31P and 63,65Cu NMR study
item.dc:creator: Riho Rästa, Ivo Heinmaa, Joosep Link, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Anomalous Hall effect in the collinear ferrimagnets GdMn2Ge2 and TbMn2Ge2

Last authors: Yu Zhang, Tian-Long Xia

Authors: Yi-Ting Wang, Xiao-Yan Wang, Huan Wang, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental transport study of anomalous Hall effect in rare-earth based magnetic materials.

Tags: #AHE #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Yi-Ting Wang, Xiao-Yan Wang, Huan Wang, Kun Han, Junfa Lin, Xue Dong, Yu Zhang, and Tian-Long Xia Rare earth-transition metal compounds $R{\mathrm{Mn}}_{2}{\mathrm{Ge}}_{2}$ ($R$=rare earth element) exhibit rich magnetic structures and intriguing physical phenomena, which have attracted sustained research interest. Different from the reported family members with light $R$ elements exhibiting non… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184429] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yi-Ting Wang, Xiao-Yan Wang, Huan Wang, Kun Han, Junfa Lin, Xue Dong, Yu Zhang, and Tian-Long Xia

Rare earth-transition metal compounds RMn2Ge2 (R=rare earth element) exhibit rich magnetic structures and intriguing physical phenomena, which have attracted sustained research interest. Different from the reported family members with light R elements exhibiting noncoplanar magnetic structures, GdMn…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184429] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Anomalous Hall effect in the collinear ferrimagnets GdMn2Ge2 and TbMn2Ge2
item.dc:creator: Yi-Ting Wang, Xiao-Yan Wang, Huan Wang, et al.

item.title: [9] Noncollinear antiferromagnetic structure and physical properties of CrRhAs with distorted kagome lattice
item.description:
Last authors: Lijie Hao, Peng Cheng

Authors: Chenglin Shang, Daye Xu, Bingxian Shi, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of a kagome metal, directly relevant to the user's primary research interest.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #kagome #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Chenglin Shang, Daye Xu, Bingxian Shi, Xuejuan Gui, Zhongcen Sun, Juanjuan Liu, Jinchen Wang, Hongxia Zhang, Hongliang Wang, Lijie Hao, and Peng Cheng CrRhAs was theoretically proposed to be a kagome metal with unusual magnetic ground states; however, little is known about its magnetic structure and physical properties experimentally. Here, we present an experimental investigation of CrRhAs with ZrNiAl-type structure and a distorted Cr kagome latt… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184430] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chenglin Shang, Daye Xu, Bingxian Shi, Xuejuan Gui, Zhongcen Sun, Juanjuan Liu, Jinchen Wang, Hongxia Zhang, Hongliang Wang, Lijie Hao, and Peng Cheng

CrRhAs was theoretically proposed to be a kagome metal with unusual magnetic ground states; however, little is known about its magnetic structure and physical properties experimentally. Here, we present an experimental investigation of CrRhAs with ZrNiAl-type structure and a distorted Cr kagome latt…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184430] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Noncollinear antiferromagnetic structure and physical properties of CrRhAs with distorted kagome lattice

item.title: [7] Multiple-gap weak-coupling superconductivity in the full-Heusler compound TiPd2Ga
item.description:
Last authors: Guangtong Liu, Zhi-An Ren

Authors: Lewei Chen, Menghu Zhou, Shunli Ni, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental characterization of multiple-gap superconductivity in a centrosymmetric full-Heusler compound.

Tags: #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Lewei Chen, Menghu Zhou, Shunli Ni, Yunqing Shi, Junkun Yi, Lihong Hu, Siyuan Zhou, Yadong Gu, Jihai Yuan, Haoyu He, Qingsong Liu, Zhengxin Lin, Meng Wang, Jiasheng Liu, Binbin Ruan, Fanming Qu, Guangtong Liu, and Zhi-An Ren Superconductivity was observed in the full-Heusler compound ${\mathrm{TiPd}}_{2}\mathrm{Ga}$ below 1.4 K. Polycrystalline samples of ${\mathrm{TiPd}}_{2}\mathrm{Ga}$ were prepared by the arc-melting method. ${\mathrm{TiPd}}_{2}\mathrm{Ga}$ adopts a centrosymmetric cubic structure with the space grou… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184510] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lewei Chen, Menghu Zhou, Shunli Ni, Yunqing Shi, Junkun Yi, Lihong Hu, Siyuan Zhou, Yadong Gu, Jihai Yuan, Haoyu He, Qingsong Liu, Zhengxin Lin, Meng Wang, Jiasheng Liu, Binbin Ruan, Fanming Qu, Guangtong Liu, and Zhi-An Ren

Superconductivity was observed in the full-Heusler compound TiPd2Ga below 1.4 K. Polycrystalline samples of TiPd2Ga were prepared by the arc-melting method. TiPd2Ga adopts a centrosymmetric cubic structure with the space group Fm-3m. Electrical-resistivity and magnetic-susceptibility measurements re…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184510] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Multiple-gap weak-coupling superconductivity in the full-Heusler compound TiPd2Ga
item.dc:creator: Lewei Chen, Menghu Zhou, Shunli Ni, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [7] Role of topotactic hydrogen in superconductivity of infinite-layer nickelate NdNiO2: A first-principles and variational Monte Carlo study
item.description:
Last authors: Amal Medhi, Tanusri Saha Dasgupta

Authors: Manoj Gupta, Arun Kumar Maurya, Amal Medhi, Tanusri Saha Dasgupta

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study on the electronic structure of nickelates, a material class the user follows.

Tags: #nickelate #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Manoj Gupta, Arun Kumar Maurya, Amal Medhi, and Tanusri Saha Dasgupta Employing a combination of first-principles calculations, low-energy model construction, and variational Monte Carlo solution of the ab initio derived Hubbard model, we study the effect of hydrogenation on the electronic structure and superconducting properties of an infinite-layer nickelate, ${\mat… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184511] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Manoj Gupta, Arun Kumar Maurya, Amal Medhi, and Tanusri Saha Dasgupta

Employing a combination of first-principles calculations, low-energy model construction, and variational Monte Carlo solution of the ab initio derived Hubbard model, we study the effect of hydrogenation on the electronic structure and superconducting properties of an infinite-layer nickelate, NdNiO2…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184511] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Role of topotactic hydrogen in superconductivity of infinite-layer nickelate NdNiO2: A first-principles and variational Monte Carlo study
item.dc:creator: Manoj Gupta, Arun Kumar Maurya, Amal Medhi, Tanusri Saha Dasgupta

item.title: [9] NMR evidence for an antisite-induced magnetic moment on Bi in the topological insulator heterostructure MnBi2Te4/(Bi2Te3)n
item.description:
Last authors: A. Lynnyk, P. Skupiński, K. Grasza, and M. Wójcik

Authors: R. Kalvig, E. Jedryka, A. Lynnyk, P. Skupiński, K. Grasza, and M. Wójcik

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental investigation of magnetism in MnBi2Te4, a high-interest magnetic topological insulator.

Tags: #topologicalinsulator #magnetism #MnBi2Te4

Abstract: Author(s): R. Kalvig, E. Jedryka, A. Lynnyk, P. Skupiński, K. Grasza, and M. Wójcik ${\mathrm{MnBi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$ (MBT) is the first intrinsic magnetic topological insulator, combining a topologically protected surface metallic state and intrinsic magnetic order. A structural compatibility with the nonmagnetic ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}$ (BT) parent compound gives… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184420] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): R. Kalvig, E. Jedryka, A. Lynnyk, P. Skupiński, K. Grasza, and M. Wójcik

MnBi2Te4 (MBT) is the first intrinsic magnetic topological insulator, combining a topologically protected surface metallic state and intrinsic magnetic order. A structural compatibility with the nonmagnetic Bi2Te3 (BT) parent compound gives a possibility to create MBT/BT heterostructures and manipul…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184420] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] NMR evidence for an antisite-induced magnetic moment on Bi in the topological insulator heterostructure MnBi2Te4/(Bi2Te3)n

item.title: [7] Dynamics of fractional quantum Hall liquids with a pulse at the edge
item.description:
Last authors: Chen-Xin Jiang, Zi-Xiang Hu

Authors: Jie Li, Chen-Xin Jiang, Zi-Xiang Hu

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of FQHE edge dynamics motivated by recent stroboscopic spectroscopy experiments.

Tags: #quantumHall #spectroscopy

Abstract: Author(s): Jie Li, Chen-Xin Jiang, and Zi-Xiang Hu Motivated by recent experimental advancements in scanning optical stroboscopic confocal microscopy and spectroscopy measurements, which have facilitated exceptional energy-space-time resolution for investigating edge and bulk dynamics in fractional quantum Hall systems, we formulated a model for the… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205117] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jie Li, Chen-Xin Jiang, and Zi-Xiang Hu

Motivated by recent experimental advancements in scanning optical stroboscopic confocal microscopy and spectroscopy measurements, which have facilitated exceptional energy-space-time resolution for investigating edge and bulk dynamics in fractional quantum Hall systems, we formulated a model for the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205117] Published Wed May 06, 2026

item.title: [7] Disorder-induced strong-field strong localization in two-dimensional systems
item.description:
Last authors: Yi Huang, Sankar Das Sarma

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical interpretation of recent STM experiments on Landau level phases in bilayer graphene.

Tags: #STM #graphene #Landaulevels

Abstract: Author(s): Yi Huang and Sankar Das Sarma A recent STM experiment in two-dimensional bilayer graphene [Y.-C. Tsui et al. , Nature (London) 628 , 287 (2024) ], under a strong perpendicular magnetic field, has made a direct observation of the existence of three distinct filling-factor-dependent quantum phases in the lowest Landau level: the inc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184203] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yi Huang and Sankar Das Sarma

A recent STM experiment in two-dimensional bilayer graphene [Y.-C. Tsui et al., Nature (London) 628, 287 (2024)], under a strong perpendicular magnetic field, has made a direct observation of the existence of three distinct filling-factor-dependent quantum phases in the lowest Landau level: the inc…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184203] Published Tue May 05, 2026

item.title: [10] Intrinsic negative magnetoresistance in layered antiferromagnetic semimetals: The case of EuSn2As2
item.description:
Last authors: V. A. Golyashov, V. M. Pudalov

Authors: K. S. Pervakov, A. V. Sadakov, O. A. Sobolevskiy, et al.

Score 10/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: Direct hit: combines ARPES, transport, and DFT on a relevant layered magnetic topological semimetal.

Tags: #ARPES #topologicalsemimetal #magnetism #transport

Abstract: Author(s): K. S. Pervakov, A. V. Sadakov, O. A. Sobolevskiy, V. A. Vlasenko, V. P. Martovitsky, E. A. Sedov, E. I. Maltsev, P. D. Grigoriev, N. S. Pavlov, I. A. Nekrasov, O. E. Tereshchenko, V. A. Golyashov, and V. M. Pudalov Here, by applying a comprehensive approach including magnetic, transport measurements, ARPES band structure measurements, DFT calculations, and analytical theory consideration, we unveil the puzzling origin of the negative isotropic magnetoresistance in the highly anisotropic semimetals, particularl… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205111] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): K. S. Pervakov, A. V. Sadakov, O. A. Sobolevskiy, V. A. Vlasenko, V. P. Martovitsky, E. A. Sedov, E. I. Maltsev, P. D. Grigoriev, N. S. Pavlov, I. A. Nekrasov, O. E. Tereshchenko, V. A. Golyashov, and V. M. Pudalov

Here, by applying a comprehensive approach including magnetic, transport measurements, ARPES band structure measurements, DFT calculations, and analytical theory consideration, we unveil the puzzling origin of the negative isotropic magnetoresistance in the highly anisotropic semimetals, particularl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205111] Published Tue May 05, 2026

item.title: [9] Emergent one-dimensional Luttinger liquid states at charge density wave domain walls embedded in monolayer 1T−VSe2
item.description:
Last authors: Lijie Zhang, Zhihui Qin

Authors: Chen Zhang, Sahr Izadi Vishkayi, Qiwei Tian, et al.

Score 9/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: title strong match: Luttinger liquid

Tags: #chargedensitywave #Luttinger #monolayer #correlated

Abstract: Author(s): Chen Zhang, Sahr Izadi Vishkayi, Qiwei Tian, Yulong Zeng, Bo Li, Linghui Tong, Yang Zhang, Li Zhang, Yuan Tian, Long-Jing Yin, Meysam Bagheri Tagani, Lijie Zhang, and Zhihui Qin Beyond the Landau Fermi liquid paradigm, non-Fermi liquid behavior stands as the cornerstone of deciphering emergent phenomena in strongly correlated quantum systems. In one-dimensional (1D) systems, the Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid (TLL) theory predicts exotic collective excitations and spin-charge se… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205113] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chen Zhang, Sahr Izadi Vishkayi, Qiwei Tian, Yulong Zeng, Bo Li, Linghui Tong, Yang Zhang, Li Zhang, Yuan Tian, Long-Jing Yin, Meysam Bagheri Tagani, Lijie Zhang, and Zhihui Qin

Beyond the Landau Fermi liquid paradigm, non-Fermi liquid behavior stands as the cornerstone of deciphering emergent phenomena in strongly correlated quantum systems. In one-dimensional (1D) systems, the Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid (TLL) theory predicts exotic collective excitations and spin-charge se…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205113] Published Tue May 05, 2026

item.title: [8] Sliding-driven switchable layer-polarized anomalous Hall effect in inversion-symmetric van der Waals systems
item.description:
Last authors: Yehui Zhang, Liang Ma

Authors: Xinyang Li, Yehui Zhang, Liang Ma

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Investigates layer-polarized anomalous Hall effects in vdW systems, which aligns with the user's topology interests.

Tags: #anomalousHall #vanderWaals #topology

Abstract: Author(s): Xinyang Li, Yehui Zhang, and Liang Ma The layer-polarized anomalous Hall effect (LP-AHE), observed in topological systems and inversion-asymmetric valleytronic systems, has attracted significant interest. However, its practical application is limited by stringent requirements of either a continuously applied external electric field or t… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205409] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xinyang Li, Yehui Zhang, and Liang Ma

The layer-polarized anomalous Hall effect (LP-AHE), observed in topological systems and inversion-asymmetric valleytronic systems, has attracted significant interest. However, its practical application is limited by stringent requirements of either a continuously applied external electric field or t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205409] Published Tue May 05, 2026

item.title: [7] Spiral spin liquid resilient to quantization in the frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet GdZnPO
item.description:
Last authors: Yi Cui, Yuesheng Li

Authors: Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental NMR study of a frustrated honeycomb lattice showing spin liquid behavior.

Tags: #spinliquid #magnetism #NMR

Abstract: Author(s): Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, Haijun Liao, Weiqiang Yu, Yi Cui, and Yuesheng Li Spin liquids usually emerge in low-spin ( S =½ or 1) systems with strong quantum fluctuations. Here, the authors show that GdZnPO—a structurally disorder-free, high-spin ( S =7⁄2) frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet—defies this expectation. Nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) measurements reveal persistent spin dynamics and spin-liquid behavior down to the lowest accessible temperatures in GdZnPO. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174402] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, Haijun Liao, Weiqiang Yu, Yi Cui, and Yuesheng Li

Spin liquids usually emerge in low-spin (S=½ or 1) systems with strong quantum fluctuations. Here, the authors show that GdZnPO—a structurally disorder-free, high-spin (S=7⁄2) frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet—defies this expectation. Nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) measurements reveal persistent spin dynamics and spin-liquid behavior down to the lowest accessible temperatures in GdZnPO.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174402] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Spiral spin liquid resilient to quantization in the frustrated honeycomb antiferromagnet GdZnPO
item.dc:creator: Xun Chen, Rui Bian, Yuqian Zhao, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Mechanism of anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in quasi-two-dimensional MnAlGe epitaxial thin films
item.description:
Last authors: Taisuke T. Sasaki, Yuya Sakuraba

Authors: Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental and theoretical study of Berry curvature and anomalous Hall effect in thin films.

Tags: #Berrycurvature #anomalousHall #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, Benugopal Bairagya, Keisuke Masuda, Weinan Zhou, Taisuke T. Sasaki, and Yuya Sakuraba We report a comprehensive experimental and theoretical study of the anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in (001)-oriented epitaxial MnAlGe thin films. First-principles calculations of intrinsic anomalous Hall conductivity originating from Berry curvature $({σ}_{xy}^{int})$ predict a large value, $∼856… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184404] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, Benugopal Bairagya, Keisuke Masuda, Weinan Zhou, Taisuke T. Sasaki, and Yuya Sakuraba

We report a comprehensive experimental and theoretical study of the anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in (001)-oriented epitaxial MnAlGe thin films. First-principles calculations of intrinsic anomalous Hall conductivity originating from Berry curvature (σxyint) predict a large value, ∼856Scm−1, at 0…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184404] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Mechanism of anomalous Hall and Nernst effects in quasi-two-dimensional MnAlGe epitaxial thin films
item.dc:creator: Nanhe Kumar Gupta, Ryo Toyama, Fuya Makino, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Competing magnetic phases in Cr3+δTe4 are spatially segregated
item.description:
Last authors: Samaresh Guchhait, J. M. Tranquada

Authors: V. K. Bhartiya, Anirban Goswami, Nicholas Ng, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of a self-intercalated van der Waals system with tunable topological properties.

Tags: #vanderWaals #magnetism #topological

Abstract: Author(s): V. K. Bhartiya, Anirban Goswami, Nicholas Ng, Wei Tian, Matthew G. Tucker, Niraj Aryal, Lijun Wu, Weiguo Yin, Yimei Zhu, Milinda Abeykoon, Emmanuel Yakubu, Samaresh Guchhait, and J. M. Tranquada ${\mathrm{Cr}}_{1+x}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2}$ is a self-intercalated van der Waals system that is of current interest for its room-temperature room-temperature ferromagnetic (FM) phases and tunable topological properties. In bulk samples, the strain from the interstitial Cr ions leads to distinct structura… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184406] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): V. K. Bhartiya, Anirban Goswami, Nicholas Ng, Wei Tian, Matthew G. Tucker, Niraj Aryal, Lijun Wu, Weiguo Yin, Yimei Zhu, Milinda Abeykoon, Emmanuel Yakubu, Samaresh Guchhait, and J. M. Tranquada

Cr1+xTe2 is a self-intercalated van der Waals system that is of current interest for its room-temperature room-temperature ferromagnetic (FM) phases and tunable topological properties. In bulk samples, the strain from the interstitial Cr ions leads to distinct structural phases for different ranges …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184406] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Competing magnetic phases in Cr3+δTe4 are spatially segregated

item.title: [7] Tunable magnetic excitations in the honeycomb antiferromagnet (Co,Ni)TiO3
item.description:
Last authors: Stuart Calder, Despina Louca

Authors: Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, Despina Louca

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Neutron scattering study of magnetic excitations in a honeycomb lattice antiferromagnet.

Tags: #neutronscattering #magnetism #honeycomb

Abstract: Author(s): Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, and Despina Louca The solid solution of the honeycomb antiferromagnet (AFM) ${\mathrm{Co}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{TiO}}_{3}$ (CNTO) was synthesized by mixing a 1:1 molar ratio of ${\mathrm{CoTiO}}_{3}$ (CTO) and ${\mathrm{NiTiO}}_{3}$ (NTO). Powder neutron scattering was used to determine the structure and … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184412] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, and Despina Louca

The solid solution of the honeycomb antiferromagnet (AFM) Co0.5Ni0.5TiO3 (CNTO) was synthesized by mixing a 1:1 molar ratio of CoTiO3 (CTO) and NiTiO3 (NTO). Powder neutron scattering was used to determine the structure and magnetic spectrum, where the nuclear and magnetic structures resemble those …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184412] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Tunable magnetic excitations in the honeycomb antiferromagnet (Co,Ni)TiO3
item.dc:creator: Srimal Rathnayaka, Luke Daemen, Stuart Calder, Despina Louca
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Theoretical design of the large topological magnetoelectric effect in the Co-intercalated NbS2 structure
item.description:
Last authors: Hyowon Park, Ivar Martin

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of topological effects in an intercalated TMD related to user's materials focus.

Tags: #topological #anomalousHall #TMD

Abstract: Author(s): Hyowon Park and Ivar Martin A triangular Co-ion lattice intercalated between 1-H ${\mathrm{NbS}}_{2}$ layers can exhibit a large anomalous Hall effect (AHE) due to the finite scalar spin chirality originating from the noncoplanar $3q$ ordering of Co spins. This large AHE occurs when the scalar spin chirality is uniform in all … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184413] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hyowon Park and Ivar Martin

A triangular Co-ion lattice intercalated between 1-H NbS2 layers can exhibit a large anomalous Hall effect (AHE) due to the finite scalar spin chirality originating from the noncoplanar 3q ordering of Co spins. This large AHE occurs when the scalar spin chirality is uniform in all Co layers, as inde…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184413] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Theoretical design of the large topological magnetoelectric effect in the Co-intercalated NbS2 structure
item.dc:creator: Hyowon Park, Ivar Martin
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Quasiclassical theory of vortex states in locally noncentrosymmetric superconductors: Application to CeRh2As2
item.description:
Last authors: Akihiro Minamide, Youichi Yanase

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory applied to a topical heavy-fermion unconventional superconductor.

Tags: #superconductivity #heavyfermion

Abstract: Author(s): Akihiro Minamide and Youichi Yanase ${\mathrm{CeRh}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$, a heavy-fermion superconductor discovered in 2021, exhibits two distinct superconducting phases under a $c$-axis magnetic field. This unconventional phase diagram has been attributed to the local inversion symmetry breaking at the $\mathrm{Ce}$ sites. At low m… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184503] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Akihiro Minamide and Youichi Yanase

CeRh2As2, a heavy-fermion superconductor discovered in 2021, exhibits two distinct superconducting phases under a c-axis magnetic field. This unconventional phase diagram has been attributed to the local inversion symmetry breaking at the Ce sites. At low magnetic fields, a conventional even-parity …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184503] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Quasiclassical theory of vortex states in locally noncentrosymmetric superconductors: Application to CeRh2As2
item.dc:creator: Akihiro Minamide, Youichi Yanase
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: [8] Unconventional superconductivity in La3Ni2O7 from the perspective of symmetry
item.description:
Last authors: Jun Quan, Yusheng Hou

Authors: Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, Yusheng Hou

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Symmetry analysis of the high-Tc nickelate superconductor La3Ni2O7.

Tags: #nickelate #unconventionalsuperconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, and Yusheng Hou The recently discovered superconductor ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$ has attracted significant attention due to its remarkably high transition temperature (${T}_{c}$) under high pressure. Shortly after this discovery, thin-film ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184505] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, and Yusheng Hou

The recently discovered superconductor La3Ni2O7 has attracted significant attention due to its remarkably high transition temperature (Tc) under high pressure. Shortly after this discovery, thin-film La3Ni2O7 was demonstrated to exhibit ambient-pressure superconductivity; however, the corresponding …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184505] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Unconventional superconductivity in La3Ni2O7 from the perspective of symmetry
item.dc:creator: Guan-Hao Feng, Jun Quan, Yusheng Hou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
